fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Michigan Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Michigan - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity michigan, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to michigan, pharmacy technician compact michigan, michigan pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: Michigan Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: Michigan Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs (LARA)

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in Michigan.

steps.title: Meet Eligibility Requirements
steps.description: You must be at least 18 years old, have a high school diploma or equivalent, and be of good moral character.

steps.title: Complete the Application
steps.description: Submit a completed application for registration as a Pharmacy Technician to the Michigan Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Pay the Required Fee
steps.description: Pay the required registration fee to the Michigan Board of Pharmacy.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the PTCB. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Michigan pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: Michigan lists a $137.70 pharmacy technician application and initial licensure fee, with a $61.20 renewal fee for the two-year cycle.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Michigan have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Michigan. To work in Michigan, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Michigan?
faqs.answer: Michigan’s current licensing report says pharmacy technician applications average about 90 days from receipt to issuance.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Michigan while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Michigan’s current licensing report says pharmacy technician applications average about 90 days from receipt to issuance.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in Michigan
hero.dek: Michigan Pharmacy Technician registration requires completing an application, meeting education or experience requirements, and paying the required fee.
